evals.name: security-changes
evals.expected_output: The agent should identify this as a Security category change (new token validation logic and role-based permission checks) and include it in the review guide. The guide should be appended to the PR description using the exact HTML comment markers <!-- pr-human-guide --> / <!-- /pr-human-guide -->. The terminal output should report 1 item in 1 category.

assertions.text: The review guide includes a Security section flagging the auth middleware changes
The review guide is wrapped in <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers
The flagged file entry includes a GitHub diff link to src/auth/middleware.ts
The skill calls gh pr edit to update the PR body with the review guide

evals.name: config-changes
evals.prompt:
Before I request review on PR #55, can you prep the description with a guide calling out what needs careful human review?

PR #55 — 'Updates deployment pipeline and IAM permissions for production'
URL: https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/55
Current PR description: 'Updates deployment pipeline and IAM permissions for production.'

Diff:

```diff
diff --git a/.github/workflows/deploy.yml b/.github/workflows/deploy.yml
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/deploy.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/deploy.yml
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@ jobs:
   deploy:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
-      - name: Deploy to staging
+      - name: Deploy to production
         uses: actions/checkout@v3
+        env:
+          AWS_REGION: us-east-1
+          DEPLOY_ENV: production
diff --git a/deploy/terraform/main.tf b/deploy/terraform/main.tf
index 9876543..fedcba9 100644
--- a/deploy/terraform/main.tf
+++ b/deploy/terraform/main.tf
@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@ resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "app_policy" {
   policy = jsonencode({
     Statement = [{
       Effect   = "Allow"
-      Action   = ["s3:GetObject"]
+      Action   = ["s3:GetObject", "s3:PutObject", "s3:DeleteObject"]
       Resource = "arn:aws:s3:::my-app-bucket/*"
     }]
   })
```
evals.expected_output: The agent should identify Config/Infrastructure changes (CI/CD workflow changing from staging to production, IAM permissions being widened). Both files should appear in the review guide. The IAM change may also appear under Security given the permissions widening.

assertions.text: The review guide includes a Config / Infrastructure section
The review guide flags the IAM permissions widening in deploy/terraform/main.tf
The review guide flags the GitHub Actions workflow change from staging to production
The skill calls gh pr edit to update the PR body with the review guide

evals.name: new-dependency
evals.expected_output: The agent should flag New Dependencies (node-forge is a cryptography library, aws-sdk provides network/cloud access — both elevated concern). The encryption.ts changes should also appear under Security (new crypto usage) and possibly Novel Patterns (first crypto module).

assertions.text: The review guide includes a New Dependencies section flagging node-forge and/or aws-sdk
The review guide specifically flags node-forge as a cryptography-related dependency warranting review
The new encryption.ts file is flagged under Security or Novel Patterns
The review guide is wrapped in <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers

evals.name: novel-pattern
evals.expected_output: The agent should identify this as a Novel Patterns change — the codebase uses try/catch with thrown AppError objects throughout, but this file introduces Result types (ts-results), which is a fundamentally different error handling strategy. The guide should explain the pattern contrast.

assertions.text: The review guide includes a Novel Patterns section flagging the Result type pattern
The review guide describes the contrast between the new Result type approach and the existing try/catch pattern used elsewhere
The review guide is wrapped in <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers

evals.name: no-special-areas
evals.expected_output: The agent should not flag any areas for special human review. This is routine UI component work with a corresponding test — no security, config, dependency, data model, novel pattern, or concurrency concerns. The review guide should output the 'no areas requiring special human review attention' message, wrapped in the exact <!-- pr-human-guide --> / <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers.

assertions.text: The review guide does not include any category sections (Security, Config, etc.) because no areas warranted flagging
The review guide body contains the message indicating no areas requiring special human review were identified
The PR description update uses the exact markers <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> (not alternative formats like <!-- REVIEW-GUIDE:START --> or similar)

evals.name: idempotent-rerun
evals.expected_output: The agent should detect the existing <!-- pr-human-guide --> block and replace it with a fresh guide rather than appending a second block. The updated guide should reflect the current diff. The PR description should contain exactly one <!-- pr-human-guide --> block after the update. The terminal output should use the exact format 'Review guide updated on PR #42'.

assertions.text: The updated PR description contains exactly one <!-- pr-human-guide --> block, not two
The replacement guide reflects the current state of the PR diff, not the stale previous guide
The terminal output contains the exact phrase 'Review guide updated on PR #' (not just general prose like 'the guide has been updated')

evals.name: data-model-changes
evals.prompt:
I'm getting PR #73 ready for review. Can you add a guide to the description flagging what needs careful attention?

PR #73 — 'User profile schema migration'
URL: https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/73
Current PR description: 'Migrates user profile schema to v2 — renames username field and removes legacy data.'

Diff:

```diff
diff --git a/migrations/20240312_user_profile_v2.sql b/migrations/20240312_user_profile_v2.sql
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/migrations/20240312_user_profile_v2.sql
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+-- Migrate user profile schema to v2
+ALTER TABLE users RENAME COLUMN username TO display_name;
+ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN legacy_id;
+ALTER TABLE users ALTER COLUMN bio SET NOT NULL;
diff --git a/schema.graphql b/schema.graphql
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/schema.graphql
+++ b/schema.graphql
@@ -8,7 +8,6 @@ type User {
   id: ID!
   email: String!
-  username: String!
+  displayName: String!
-  legacyId: Int
   bio: String
 }
```
evals.expected_output: The agent should identify Data Model Changes: the RENAME COLUMN is a potentially breaking change if any clients reference 'username' directly, the DROP COLUMN is destructive, the SET NOT NULL on an existing column can fail if nulls exist, and the GraphQL schema removes the 'username' field and 'legacyId' field which breaks existing API consumers. All should appear under Data Model Changes.

assertions.text: The review guide includes a Data Model Changes section
The review guide flags the RENAME COLUMN (username to display_name) or the GraphQL field rename as a potential breaking change for existing clients
The review guide flags the DROP COLUMN or GraphQL field removal as a destructive/breaking operation
The review guide is wrapped in <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers

evals.name: concurrency-state
evals.expected_output: The agent should identify Concurrency / State concerns: activeJobCount and jobQueue are module-level shared mutable state with no lock protection, and worker threads are being introduced for the first time. This also qualifies as Novel Patterns (first use of worker threads). Both categories should appear in the guide.

assertions.text: The review guide includes a Concurrency / State section
The review guide flags the module-level shared mutable state (activeJobCount and/or jobQueue) as a concurrency concern
The review guide flags the new use of worker threads
The review guide is wrapped in <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers
